pack_purpose: Deterministic narrow follow-up pack for targeted escalation. It defines the crux questions and operational budgets, while source choice may remain model-driven within runtime-enforced tool guardrails.
verification_questions: Does the latest available information materially change the decision, given: BTC can reprice several percent in under three days and the contract resolves on one exact Apr 17 noon ET minute?
How decision-relevant is the remaining contract/source-of-truth ambiguity: narrow minute-close/timezone/venue mechanics create small operational interpretation risk but rules are mostly explicit?
Are the current blockers still material to final judgment: No fresh synthesis-stage live price/ladder refresh beyond upstream checks?
Is the main verification gap still decision-material: No fresh independent live market or volatility check beyond upstream lane verification was obtained in synthesis.?

structured_handoff_focus.contract_ambiguity_reason: narrow minute-close/timezone/venue mechanics create small operational interpretation risk but rules are mostly explicit
structured_handoff_focus.verification_gap_summary: No fresh independent live market or volatility check beyond upstream lane verification was obtained in synthesis.
structured_handoff_focus.best_countercase_summary: A normal crypto selloff or badly timed noon-minute wick on Binance can still flip this narrow contract despite spot being comfortably above 70k now.
structured_handoff_focus.main_reason_for_disagreement: how much probability discount to apply for exact-minute Binance settlement and ordinary 3-day BTC volatility
structured_handoff_focus.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes requires the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle closing at 12:00 PM ET on Apr 17 to finish strictly above 70000.
structured_handoff_focus.freshness_driver: BTC can reprice several percent in under three days and the contract resolves on one exact Apr 17 noon ET minute
decision_blockers: No fresh synthesis-stage live price/ladder refresh beyond upstream checks
Exact-minute Binance settlement structure leaves residual path and venue-specific risk
Apparent edge versus market is modest and not strongly independently verified

supporting_persona_summaries.main_thesis: The market's high-probability Yes view is broadly efficient because Binance BTC/USDT is currently well above 70k, but the 93% price is slightly rich once short-horizon volatility and the single-minute settlement rule are fully accounted for.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market's 92.5%-93% Yes prior because the strike ladder contains information about the expected BTC distribution.
Check the governing source directly: Binance spot and recent klines show BTC materially above 70000 and trading in a regime where 70000 is below the recent center of price action.
Apply a modest discount for ordinary crypto volatility and the narrow single-minute settlement mechanic, yielding an 89% Yes estimate rather than the full market price.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot was about 74293.57, roughly 6.1% above the 70000 threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes were mostly above 70000, with recent highs above 76000.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can fall more than 6% in three days.
The contract settles on a single Binance 1-minute close at noon ET, adding timing/path risk.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether short-horizon macro or crypto-specific news hits before the Apr 17 noon ET settlement window.
How much extra probability mass the market is assigning to current regime persistence versus pure spot anchoring.
supporting_persona_summaries.what_would_change_view: I would move materially lower if Binance BTCUSDT fell back toward or below 72k, if neighboring strikes repriced sharply lower, or if Binance-specific settlement-risk issues emerged.
supporting_persona_summaries.timing_relevance: Very high: the contract resolves on the Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle close at 12:00 PM ET on 2026-04-17, so both timezone and single-minute timing matter materially.
supporting_persona_summaries.source_quality_view: High-quality direct evidence from Binance plus clear contract mechanics from Polymarket; independence is medium because sources reflect the same underlying BTC market, with CoinGecko adding only a modest contextual cross-check.
